volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 3.2.553 - Section 3.3.604)

sections.label: Section 3.2.553
sections.translation: The necessity will not arise , I trust .

sections.label: Section 3.2.553b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and I trust so too : nor do I say so now , because I have suspected him in any way ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.555
sections.original: Sed si quid , ne quid . Quae sit eius aetas vides .
sections.translation: but in case , none the more [*] — You see what his age is ; ( aside ) and truly , Chremes , [*] if an occasion does happen , I may be able to handle you right handsomely .

sections.label: Section 3.2.557b
sections.translation: As to that , we ’ ll consider what is requisite when the occasion does happen . At present do you set about this matter .

sections.label: Section 3.2.559
sections.original: Nunquam commodius unquam herum audivi loqui ,
sections.translation: Never on any occasion did I hear my master talk more to the purpose ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.560
sections.original: Nec quum male facerem crederem mihi impunius
sections.translation: nor at any time could I believe that I was authorized to play the rogue with greater impunity . I wonder who it is coming out of our house ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.562
sections.original: Quid istuc , quaeso ? qui istic mos est , Clitipho ? itane fieri oportet ?
sections.translation: Pray , what does this mean ? What behavior is this , Clitipho ? Is this acting as becomes you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.563
sections.translation: What have I done ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.563b
sections.original: Vidine ego te modo manum in sinum huic meretrici
sections.translation: Did I not see you just now putting your hand into this Courtesan ’ s bosom ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.564b
sections.translation: It ’ s all up with us — I ’ m utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 3.3.564c
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.564d
sections.translation: With these self-same eyes I saw it — don ’ t deny it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.565
sections.original: Facis adeo indigne iniuriam illi , qui non abstineas manum :
sections.translation: Besides , you wrong him unworthily in not keeping your hands off : for indeed it is a gross affront to entertain a person , your friend , at your house , and to take liberties with his mistress . Yesterday , for instance , at wine , how rude you were —

sections.label: Section 3.3.568b
sections.translation: ’ Tis the truth [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.568c
sections.translation: How annoying you were ! So much so , that for my part , as the Gods may prosper me , I dreaded what in the end might be the consequence .

sections.label: Section 3.3.570
sections.original: Novi ego amantium animum : advertunt graviter quae non censeas .
sections.translation: I understand lovers . They resent highly things that you would not imagine .

sections.label: Section 3.3.571
sections.original: At mihi fides apud hunc est nihil me istius facturum , pater .
sections.translation: But he has full confidence in me , father , that I would not do any thing of that kind .

sections.label: Section 3.3.572
sections.original: Esto : at certe concedas hinc aliquo ab ore eorum aliquantisper .
sections.translation: Be it so ; still , at least , you ought to go somewhere for a little time away from their presence . Passion prompts to many a thing ; your presence acts as a restraint upon doing them . I form a judgment from myself . There ’ s not one of my friends this day

sections.label: Section 3.3.575
sections.original: Apud quem expromere omnia mea occulta , Clitipho , audeam ;
sections.translation: to whom I would venture , Clitipho , to disclose all my secrets . With one , his station forbids it ; with another , I am ashamed of the action itself , lest I may appear a fool or devoid of shame ; do you rest assured that he does the same . [*] But it is our part to be sensible of this ; and , when and where it is requisite , to show due complaisance .

sections.label: Section 3.3.579
sections.translation: What is it he is saying ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.579b
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 3.3.579c
sections.translation: Clitipho , these same injunctions I gave you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.580
sections.original: Hominis frugi et temperantis functus es officium ?
sections.translation: You have acted the part of a prudent and discreet person . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.580b
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , I beg .

sections.label: Section 3.3.581
sections.translation: Very good .

sections.label: Section 3.3.581b
sections.translation: Syrus , I am ashamed of him .

sections.label: Section 3.3.581c
sections.translation: I believe it ; and not without reason . Why , he vexes myself even .

sections.label: Section 3.3.582b
sections.translation: Do you persist , then ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.582c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m saying the truth , as it appears to me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.583
sections.translation: May I not go near them ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.583b
sections.original: Eho quaeso , una accedendi via est ?
sections.translation: How now — pray , is there but one way [*] of going near them ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.584
sections.original: Actum est : hic prius se indicarit quam ego argentum effecero .
sections.translation: Confusion ! He ’ ll be betraying himself before I ’ ve got the money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.585
sections.original: Chreme , vin tu homini stulto mihi auscultare ?
sections.translation: ( Aloud .) Chremes , will you give attention to me , who am but a silly person ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.585b
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.585c
sections.translation: Bid him go somewhere out of the way .

sections.label: Section 3.3.586b
sections.translation: Where am I to go ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.586c
sections.original: Quo ? quo libet : da illis locum .
sections.translation: Where you please ; leave the place to them ; be off and take a walk .

sections.label: Section 3.3.587b
sections.translation: Take a walk ! where ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.587c
sections.translation: Pshaw ! Just as if there was no place to walk in . Why , then , go this way , that way , where you will .

sections.label: Section 3.3.588b
sections.translation: He says right , I ’ m of his opinion .

sections.label: Section 3.3.589
sections.original: Di te eradicent , Syre , qui me hinc extrudis .
sections.translation: May the Gods extirpate you , Syrus , for thrusting me away from here .

sections.label: Section 3.3.589b
sections.translation: Then

sections.label: Section 3.3.590
sections.original: Tu pol tibi istas posthac comprimito manus .
sections.translation: do you for the future keep those hands of yours within bounds . ( Exit CLITIPHO .) Really now ( to CHREMES ) , what do you think ? What do you imagine will become of him next , unless , so far as the Gods afford you the means , you watch him , correct and admonish him ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.593
sections.translation: I ’ ll take care of that .

sections.label: Section 3.3.593b
sections.original: Atqui nunc , here , hic tibi asservandus est .
sections.translation: But now , master , he must be looked after by you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.594
sections.translation: It shall be done .

sections.label: Section 3.3.594b
sections.original: Si sapias : nam mihi iam minus minusque obtemperat .
sections.translation: If you are wise ,— for now he minds me less and less every day .

sections.label: Section 3.3.595
sections.original: Quid tu ? ecquid de illo quod dudum tecum egi egisti , Syre ? aut
sections.translation: What say you ? What have you done , Syrus , about that matter which I was mentioning to you a short time since ? Have you any plan that suits you , or not yet even ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.596b
sections.translation: You mean the design upon Menedemus ? I have ; I have just hit upon one .

sections.label: Section 3.3.597b
sections.translation: You are a clever fellow ; what is it ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.598
sections.original: Dicam : verum , ut aliud ex alio incidit .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; but , as one matter arises out of another —

sections.label: Section 3.3.598b
sections.translation: Why , what . is it , Syrus ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.599
sections.translation: This Courtesan is a very bad woman .

sections.label: Section 3.3.599b
sections.translation: So she seems .

sections.label: Section 3.3.599c
sections.translation: Aye , if you did but know .

sections.label: Section 3.3.600
sections.original: Vah , vide , quod inceptet facinus . Fuit quaedam anus Corinthia
sections.translation: O shocking ! just see what she is hatching . There was a certain old woman here from Corinth ,— this Bacchis lent her a thousand silver drachmae .

sections.label: Section 3.3.602
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.602b
sections.original: Ea mortua est : reliquit filiam adolescentulam .
sections.translation: She is now dead : she has left a daughter , a young girl . She has been left with this Bacchis as a pledge for that sum .

sections.label: Section 3.3.603b
sections.translation: I understand you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.604
sections.original: Hanc secum huc adduxit , ea quae est nunc apud uxorem tuam .
sections.translation: She has brought her hither along with her , her I mean who is now with your wife . [*]
